text:
At this time, the world is ordered by memory, ritual
and tradition rather than formal doctrine. Knowledge is
preserved in temple archives, carried in the speeches of
orators and guarded within noble households. The motions
of the heavens are observed but not yet measured; numbers
are tools of trade and tribute; law is recited before it
is written.

With Academics, you possess a cultivated understanding of
history, rhetoric, philosophy, mathematics, and sacred custom.
You are trained in the disciplines that shape cities and empires
--the power of words, the foundation of knowledge and the wisdom
of those who came before. In an age where most learn only what
they are told, you seek to know why.
HR:
Each level of academics affords you one field of expertise.
You can buy additional fields for 2 XP or 1 freebie pt each.
Fields of expertise must be notated using +notes.
Characters with academics 1+ are literate.
Characters without academics are illiterate.
title: Academics

text:
Enigmas encompass symbolism, history, lore, and cryptography.
The smallest pieces click into a cohesive whole when you
have sufficient knowledge of enigmas.
Each thread spins together into a tapestry of an answer.

Your experience with enigmas allows you to draw
logical connections to deduce the solution when it is least
evident to others.
title: Enigmas

text:
What berries can you eat? Will it rain tomorrow? What's
the best remedy for whooping cough? This wisdom is not found
in books, but from sitting at the knee of people wiser than you,
and spoken in sewing circles and market stalls. They know
the cures and they know the reasons to be afraid of the dark.
title: Hearth Wisdom

text:
Your eye spies the smallest details, tracing them back
through the web of information. You have the questions
and they have the answers, and woe betide any who stand
long in the way of your search.

You connect all the threads, weaving them together
to reveal the tapestry of the truth. Or the gods' truth.
Or the Senate's truth. Whatever truth you seek, you have
the tools to discover it.
title: Investigation

text:
The Romans called it Justitia, but you don't need Latin
to know how it works. Those who have the purse to pay
receive finer justice than those who don't; the patrician
trumps the plebeian and the plebeian trumps the slave.
title: Law

text:
The oil that allows the gears of a great house to run
smoothly. The knowledge of handling books and the market
accounts. Management of number of workers for the harvest,
maintain the stables, keep the ponds and forests stocked,
and so forth.
HR: The name of 'Seneschal' was replaced for personal reasons.
title: Logistics

text:
This is an age when illness is as often blamed on offended
spirits as on natural causes. Healing belongs to midwives,
temple attendants, herbalists, and physicians trained
through apprenticeship rather than formal study. Remedies
are drawn from roots, resins, wine, honey, and poultices;
prayers and offerings may accompany treatment as readily as
bandages. Knowledge of the body comes from wounds sustained in
battle and the tending of livestock, not careful dissection.
Bones are set, fevers cooled, and bleeding stanched, but the
deeper workings of flesh and breath remain mysterious. In the
meantime, drink the bitter draught, trust in the gods, and hope
the sickness passes.
title: Medicine

text:
To know the occult is to understand the unseen and
the inexplicable. The supernatural is a hole that reaches
much deeper and darker than people imagine. Just as you
reach the bottom, another tunnel reveals itself, burrowing
ever deeper.

Some might say Occult and Theology are just two
sides of a coin that reality flips. One person's miracle is
another person's ghostly encounter. Both require a faith
in something not easily seen or understood. Overlap does
occur between the two, especially in the case of miracles
or saints.

If a character's Occult score is high, the outcomes of
their Occult rolls tend to be more accurate.
HR:
Each level of occult affords you one field of expertise.
You can buy additional fields for 2 XP or 1 freebie pt each.
Fields of expertise must be notated using +notes.
title: Occult

text:
Politics is the great game; some say the greatest game. It
can bring unimaginable power, but the wheel of fortune can
turn quickly, grinding the ambitious into the finest of powder.
History is littered with the names of those who overreached.

High-level politics is a mixture of diplomacy and
carefully exercised leverage. The more you understand,
the more you can bend others to achieve your goals.
title: Politics

text:
Theology is the science of the gods, by whatever name you
might call them, and never was it more compelling than at
this time in history. The gods were in the smallest mustard seed
and in the largest ocean. Plagues were the wrath of the gods and
a bountiful harvest were the blessings of the gods made manifest.
title: Theology
